name: RYOBI PCL520B ONE+ 18V Cordless Power Inflator

specs.Voltage: 18V ONE+ (Tool Only — battery and charger sold separately)
specs.Auto Shutoff: Yes — digital preset target pressure, automatic stop
specs.Adapters: Presta valve, ball inflation needle, air mattress nozzle included

whatWorks: Cordless 18V operation means you can inflate tires in a parking lot, a trailhead, or a remote campsite with no vehicle connection required—the defining advantage over 12V units that require a running car to operate

150 PSI capacity handles passenger car tires (typically 30–35 PSI), bicycle tires (up to 120 PSI for road bikes), and even lower-pressure SUV and truck tires in one unit—substantially more versatile than 12V inflators that max at 100 PSI

RYOBI's ONE+ battery platform compatibility means existing RYOBI tool owners can add this inflator without purchasing additional batteries—if you already own a RYOBI drill, saw, or blower, your batteries work here

The digital preset with auto-shutoff at target PSI is accurate and consistent—no watching a gauge and guessing when to stop, which matters for maintaining proper tire pressure for fuel economy and tire longevity
worthKnowing: Sold as tool-only (battery and charger not included)—buyers who don't already own RYOBI 18V batteries need to add a starter kit ($50–$80) to the purchase, making the total investment $140–$170 versus the $50 Avid Power corded alternative
The run time of 15–20 minutes per 2.0 Ah charge covers 2–3 typical car tires from low to target pressure but a full four-tire inflation from near-flat uses most of the charge
The hose is integrated and non-removable; if the hose develops a crack, the entire unit requires service rather than a simple hose replacement

commonComplaints.title: Requires separate battery purchase for new users
commonComplaints.body: The tool-only purchase price of $89 is misleading for buyers who need a battery too. A 2.0 Ah battery adds $50–$60 to the cost. The value proposition is specifically for existing RYOBI ONE+ owners.

commonComplaints.title: Run time limits full four-tire inflation on 2Ah
commonComplaints.body: A 2.0 Ah battery typically inflates 2–3 tires fully before needing a swap or recharge. Running four tires from near-flat requires a 4.0 Ah or 5.0 Ah battery for a single uninterrupted session.

commonComplaints.title: Louder than 12V compressors
commonComplaints.body: The 18V motor runs louder than typical 12V car compressors. Residents inflating bike tires in an apartment common area or early morning neighborhood use may receive complaints from the noise.

verdict: The RYOBI PCL520B is the right cordless inflator for RYOBI ONE+ tool ecosystem owners who want flexible, vehicle-independent tire inflation with 150 PSI capability. The cordless freedom and 150 PSI capacity justify the premium over 12V car port units. Non-RYOBI users who need to buy a battery kit should compare the total $140–$170 cost against an NOCO battery-free alternative or corded compressor at $50—the math changes significantly outside the ecosystem.

realBuyers.quote: I have 8 RYOBI ONE+ tools and three batteries. This inflator costs me $89 and I can inflate anything anywhere with a battery I already own. That value is undeniable.
Used this to inflate four MTB tires at a trailhead with no car outlet nearby. 150 PSI handles road bike tires too. The auto-shutoff at target PSI is perfect.
Works great. Loud, but works great. Pre-set the pressure, clip it on, walk away. Exactly what I needed.

metaDescription: RYOBI PCL520B ONE+ 18V inflator review: 150 PSI, cordless, auto-shutoff at preset pressure. Best cordless tire inflator for RYOBI ONE+ tool owners. $89 CAD (tool only).
introText: The RYOBI PCL520B ONE+ 18V Cordless Power Inflator (ASIN B08HN8FJYC) is a 150 PSI battery-powered tire inflator with digital auto-shutoff at preset target pressure, sold as a tool-only unit compatible with all RYOBI 18V ONE+ batteries. Available on Amazon.ca, it includes Presta valve, ball needle, and air mattress adapters for versatile inflation without a vehicle connection.
